findings.evidence: `rg -n "map_range_eq_bot_of_simplyConnected|fundamentalGroupMap_range_le_of_simplyConnected" code mathlib roadmap` shows the lemma is introduced at lines 45-47 and immediately used at line 61 to rewrite the range to `⊥`; Mathlib supplies `[SimplyConnectedSpace A] : Subsingleton (FundamentalGroup A a₀)` in `Mathlib/AlgebraicTopology/FundamentalGroupoid/SimplyConnected.lean:69`.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to `FundamentalGroup.map_range_eq_bot_of_simplyConnected`; then the range-inclusion lemma can remain a named convenience theorem if desired.
findings.issue: `FundamentalGroup.map_range_eq_bot_of_simplyConnected` is the normal form for the range of a fundamental-group map out of a simply connected space, but it is not marked `@[simp]`, so downstream goals like `(map f a₀).range ≤ H` will not simplify to `⊥ ≤ H`.

summary: The wrapper removal is consistent with the roadmap, but the new characteristic normal-form lemma is missing the automation annotation consumers need.
